answers: The World Health Organization (WHO) is a UN agency responsible for international public health, coordinating global responses to disease outbreaks and health emergencies.
WHO diseases er against fight kore vaccination programs, health guidelines, research funding, ar emergency response coordinate kore globally.
WHO sets global health standards, monitors disease outbreaks, provides technical assistance to countries, and leads campaigns for disease eradication like polio and malaria.
The WHO plays a critical role during pandemics by coordinating international response, sharing research data, and helping countries strengthen their healthcare systems.
